message:
feat(render): enhance presenter with adaptive line rendering and type safety

Major improvements to the Presenter for terminal output rendering, including
adaptive line emission strategies and fixes for type safety issues.

Key changes:

Adaptive line rendering:
- Implement intelligent line-by-line vs bulk emission based on content density
- Add iteration count tracking for performance monitoring
- Support configurable thresholds for switching between rendering strategies

Type safety fixes:
- Fix iterations counter type mismatch (u32 vs usize comparison at line 2573)
- Add explicit type conversions for cross-platform compatibility
- Improve bounds checking for line iteration loops

Performance improvements:
- Optimize emit_style() for common style transitions
- Reduce redundant ANSI code generation for adjacent same-style cells
- Add early-exit paths for unchanged line segments

Style handling enhancements:
- Expand support for extended color specifications
- Improve foreground/background color state tracking
- Add hyperlink sequence support (OSC 8)

The presenter now better handles:
- Very long lines with mixed styling
- Wide character (CJK) content alignment
- Terminal resize during rendering
- High-frequency update scenarios

These changes complement the dirty-row diff optimization by ensuring
efficient rendering once differences are identified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
